title: Prenatal exposure to perfluorooctanoate (PFOA) and perfluorooctanesulfonate (PFOS) and maternally reported developmental milestones in infancy.
ehr_evidence: We obtained data on Apgar scores by linking cohort members to the National Hospital Discharge Register (Copenhagen, Denmark) through use of the unique personal identification number assigned to all Danish residents at birth or immigration.
summary: A study investigating the association between prenatal exposure to perfluorooctanoate (PFOA) and perfluorooctanesulfonate (PFOS) and neurodevelopmental/musculoskeletal development (Apgar scores) in infants using the Danish National Birth Cohort.
key_findings: The study examined the association between prenatal PFOA/PFOS exposure and Apgar scores in infants.
captured_features: Maternal blood PFOA and PFOS concentrations
Apgar scores (heart rate, respiratory effort, reflex irritability, muscle tone, and skin color)
population: Pregnant women and infants in the Danish National Birth Cohort
limitations: Not specified in the provided text snippet
